problem.title: Reverse Integer
problem.description: Given a signed 32-bit integer x, return x with its digits reversed.
assumptions: Return 0 if reversed integer overflows 32-bit signed integer.

example.explanation: Reverse digits of integer. Handle overflow: return 0 if result outside 32-bit signed range [-2³¹, 2³¹-1].

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Digit by Digit
solutions.description: Extract digits one at a time by taking x % 10 and rebuild the reversed number by multiplying the running result by 10. Before each append, check whether the result would overflow the 32-bit signed integer range ([-2^31, 2^31-1]) and return 0 if it would.
solutions.code_python:
import math

def reverse(x: int) -> int:
    INT_MAX = 2**31 - 1
    INT_MIN = -(2**31)
    result = 0
    sign = -1 if x < 0 else 1
    x = abs(x)

    while x != 0:
        digit = x % 10
        x //= 10
        result = result * 10 + digit

    result *= sign
    if result < INT_MIN or result > INT_MAX:
        return 0
    return result
